title: DOM State, Events, and Accessible Interaction · Behavior Workshop
summary: Build a keyboard-operable service-status interface whose DOM, focus, announcements, and visual state stay synchronized. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an accessible interactive service-status interface while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query, create, move, replace, and remove DOM nodes with explicit missing-element and ownership handling.
Derive DOM output from application state without duplicating conflicting sources of truth.
Trace event target, currentTarget, capture, bubble, composed paths, and cancellation before delegating behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
